p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Dense foliage, yellow pot, stable posture.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Maintained consistent turgidity throughout the 5-day sequence. No significant leaf drop or color shift observed.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_inference: Stable. The plant shows high resilience to the VPD stress noted in previous reports.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Two primary leaves, central position in black pot.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: The leaf margins show persistent dehydration. The overall posture remains drooping compared to the baseline.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_inference: Stressed. Visual evidence of margin necrosis and lack of turgor pressure confirms the A5 sensor failure previously identified.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Two leaves, white rabbit scale anchor present.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: The tip necrosis on the larger leaf has remained static since T-5. No further progression of tissue death.
p3_pothos.visual_health_inference: Stable. The plant is holding its current state despite the environmental VPD challenges.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Smallest specimen, located near the rim of the black pot.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: The specimen has remained dormant with no new leaf development observed over the 5-day period.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_inference: Stressed. Growth is currently arrested; likely suffering from the same root-zone or hydration issues as p2.

biome_observations.soil_surface: The white granular material (perlite/additives) is visible across the soil surface, confirming successful user intervention as per the 'added powder' protocol.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: No weeds or uncatalogued sprouts detected in any of the four pots.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: The soil texture appears consistently dry across all pots, suggesting that the recent AC cooling trial has not yet significantly altered the evaporation rate at the soil surface.

temporal_deltas.t_minus_5_to_current: The most significant change is the introduction of white soil amendments (T-4), which have remained stable. The plant specimens have shown no recovery or decline in their respective states since the amendment.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of 'Stasis'. While the user has attempted to mitigate stress via soil amendments, the plants (specifically p2 and p4) remain in a state of physiological stress due to the persistent VPD and potential root-zone compaction.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of the white granular material is confirmed as a successful user action. No fungal growth or desk debris detected.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms that the plants are in a holding pattern. The cooling trial (AC_ON) has not yet manifested in visible physiological recovery for p2 or p4. The Pothos (p3) and String of Nickels (p1) remain the most stable. The white rabbit anchor remains undisturbed, confirming no physical displacement of the pots.
